description: A vertical text-heavy profile/story card on a dark navy-blue background watermarked with a faint Department of Labor seal. The header reads 'NATIONAL' in large white serif caps with 'APPRENTICESHIP DAY' in smaller italic beneath. Several paragraphs of small white serif body text narrate the success story of electrician Joshua Lira, who completed a five-year International Brotherhood of Electrical Workers (IBEW) Registered Apprenticeship in four years, became an 'Ultimate Journeyman,' and now enjoys a stable career bending pipe and running circuits, including the quote 'Going through the apprenticeship was the best choice I've ever made.' In the lower-right is an inset photograph of Lira, a bearded man in a gray T-shirt and safety glasses, using a flashlight while working on electrical equipment. This is a designed promotional/biographical graphic celebrating registered apprenticeships and union training, using a relatable worker success narrative on official DOL branding to encourage workforce participation. Target audience: prospective apprentices, workers, and the public.
notable_text: NATIONAL APPRENTICESHIP DAY; Electrician Joshua Lira says he learns best by doing...; 'Going through the apprenticeship was the best choice I've ever made'
